title: rosa canina subsp. tomentella
intro: Rosa canina subsp. tomentella, also known as the downy wild rose or downy dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species, native to parts of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and flavonoids, which provide various health benefits, such as supporting cardiovascular health, boosting immune function, and acting as an antiseptic. Rosa canina subsp. tomentella can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
